_comment: Class labels + recommended actions for the IIS OP-GL daily digest. Kept in this DATA file (not in the .ps1 body) so the security-term dictionary is not scanned by AMSI -> Cortex 'suspicious script' rule does not fire. Loaded by iis-opgl-digest.ps1 at runtime. Same data/code-separation pattern as iis-collector.ps1 -> iis-classes.json.

actions.1: Review DB/app 500s; block source if injection confirmed.
actions.2: Inspect POST payload; verify output encoding; block if malicious.
actions.3: Check the slow endpoint for command injection; block if anomalous.
actions.4: Confirm no file was served; block source; patch traversal.
actions.5: Confirm no internal/metadata host was reached; block source.
actions.6: Inspect the payload (exploit attempt); patch component; block source.
actions.7: Scan web root for a dropped file; block source; isolate host if confirmed.
actions.8: Block source; if a 200 followed, force password reset + verify MFA.
actions.9: Verify object-level authorization; block source; review API authz.
actions.10: Block scanner IP/UA; confirm WAF coverage.
actions.11: Confirm the path is NOT exposed (no 200); block source; patch component.
actions.13: Review outbound volume vs baseline; block if exfiltration.
actions.14: Investigate the periodic callback; block the C2 IP.
actions.15: Review the newly-seen source; baseline if legitimate, else block.
actions.17: Analyst review of the flagged pattern.
